\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
var(USSteamCoEstim$revenue) * (n_obs - 1)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 9.530679e+14
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}

\noindent This equality holds because \ttblue{var()} divides by $n - 1$. It is a useful consistency check, but the step-by-step calculation above remains the clearer way to see how Total Sum of Squares is built from the deviations around the mean.
